title: Interpret Robot Battery Thresholds For Task Acceptance And Startup Risk
summary: Use the training guidance on battery percentage thresholds to judge whether a robot is suitable for new tasking, is approaching shutdown risk, or may require manual charging handling.
when_to_use: Use when an operator needs to interpret a robot battery percentage against the training guidance for task acceptance, continued powered operation, or low-battery recovery risk.
not_for: Do not use as a precise charging or recovery procedure when the battery reading method is not defined in the source.
Do not use to infer exact robot behavior beyond the source-stated thresholds and conversational guidance.
Do not use as a substitute for manual charging handling instructions beyond what is explicitly stated in the source.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Observe the available battery percentage indication, compare it to the documented thresholds, and flag when the robot may not accept new work or may shut down.

safety_notes: Use only the source-stated battery thresholds and guidance.
Avoid letting the robot get close to 15% when possible, because the source states it may turn off automatically and cannot be turned on until taken to a charger.
If the robot is already too low to move or power on, stop normal operation and prepare for manual charging handling as described in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the robot battery percentage indication
Training guidance describing the 30%, 20%, and 15% thresholds

access_required: Ability to view the robot battery percentage indication
system_preconditions: A robot battery percentage is available from the system indication or source-provided reading method if available in context.
external_references: UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1, segment training_video_day1_segment_0050

visual_references.description: Training video frame and transcript discussing battery thresholds, manual charging handling, and low-battery recovery guidance.

steps.title: Check the robot battery percentage
steps.purpose: Establish the current battery level before interpreting readiness or low-battery risk.
steps.instruction: Check the robot battery percentage using the available system indication or source-provided battery reading method if available in context.
steps.expected_result: A current battery percentage is observed for the robot.
steps.healthy_condition: The battery percentage can be viewed and used for comparison to the training thresholds.
steps.failure_condition: The battery percentage is not available or the reading method is not defined in the source context.

actions.label: View battery percentage
actions.exact_action: Check the robot battery percentage using the available system indication or source-provided battery reading method if available in context.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Nearby transcript reference to checking the power section and HV charge percentage during troubleshooting.
Battery-threshold discussion associated with low-battery behavior and manual charging context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Battery percentage cannot be determined from the available indication.
The observed battery behavior cannot be matched to the source guidance.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Nearby transcript discusses checking the power section to see HV charge percentage such as 50% or low 30% values during troubleshooting.

steps.title: Compare the battery percentage to the documented thresholds
steps.purpose: Interpret whether the robot is still suitable for tasking or is approaching low-battery limits.
steps.instruction: Compare the observed battery percentage to the documented thresholds discussed in the training: around 30% is a point where charging should be considered when possible, under 20% the robot may not accept any new task, and at about 15% it may turn off automatically.
steps.expected_result: The operator classifies the robot as charge-soon, may-not-accept-new-tasks, or shutdown-risk based on the source guidance.
steps.healthy_condition: The battery percentage is clearly above the lower thresholds or is recognized early enough for charging attention.
steps.failure_condition: The battery percentage is under 20% or near 15%, indicating the robot may not accept new tasks or may turn off automatically.

actions.label: Interpret threshold status
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed battery percentage to the documented thresholds discussed in the training: around 30% is a point where charging should be considered when possible, under 20% the robot may not accept any new task, and at about 15% it may turn off automatically.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Transcript-supported threshold guidance: around 30% consider charging, under 20% no new task acceptance, around 15% automatic shutdown.

stop_or_escalate_if: Observed behavior does not match the training guidance for the battery range.
The operator is being asked to apply a stricter threshold or behavior not supported by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Under 20% it will not accept any new task; at 15% it will turn off automatically and cannot be turned on until taken to a charger.
If you see something at 30% when it can charge, let's just because it will cost you more time later.

steps.title: Record low-battery operational risk
steps.purpose: Document that the robot may no longer be reliable for normal tasking when near the lower thresholds.
steps.instruction: If the robot is near the lower thresholds, record that the robot may not take new work or may not stay powered on.
steps.expected_result: The robot is flagged as a low-battery operational risk based on the source guidance.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot remains above the lower-risk range and no low-battery limitation needs to be recorded.
steps.failure_condition: The robot is near or below the lower thresholds and may not accept new work or remain powered on.

actions.label: Record low-battery risk
actions.exact_action: If the robot is near the lower thresholds, record that the robot may not take new work or may not stay powered on.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Low-battery discussion tying threshold levels to task acceptance and automatic shutdown risk.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot is under 20% and is still expected to accept new tasks contrary to the source guidance.
The robot is near 15% and normal operation is still being attempted without charging attention.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Under 20% it will not accept any new task; at 15% it will turn off automatically and cannot be turned on until taken to a charger.

steps.title: Avoid letting the robot approach 15% when possible
steps.purpose: Apply the source guidance to reduce time loss and avoid automatic shutdown conditions.
steps.instruction: Use the source guidance to avoid letting the robot get close to 15% when possible.
steps.expected_result: Charging attention is considered earlier so the robot does not reach the automatic shutdown threshold.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot is identified for charging before it reaches the lowest threshold.
steps.failure_condition: The robot is allowed to approach 15%, increasing the risk of automatic shutdown and lost time.

actions.label: Apply early charging judgment
actions.exact_action: Use the source guidance to avoid letting the robot get close to 15% when possible.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Guidance that charging earlier, such as around 30%, can save time and help avoid very low battery states.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot is repeatedly being allowed to drop near 15% during normal operation.
Site behavior or system behavior conflicts with the training guidance.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If you see something at 30% when it can charge, let's just because it will cost you more time later.
Under 20% it will not accept any new task; at 15% it will turn off automatically and cannot be turned on until taken to a charger.

steps.title: Stop normal operation and prepare for manual charging handling if battery is too low
steps.purpose: Transition from normal task interpretation to low-battery recovery handling when the robot is too low to move or power on.
steps.instruction: If the robot has already dropped too low to move or power on, stop normal operation and prepare for manual charging handling as described in the source.
steps.expected_result: The operator recognizes that normal tasking is no longer appropriate and that charger-based recovery handling is needed.
steps.healthy_condition: The robot still has enough charge to remain in normal operation or to be addressed before shutdown.
steps.failure_condition: The robot has turned off automatically or cannot be turned on until taken to a charger.

actions.label: Prepare manual charging handling
actions.exact_action: If the robot has already dropped too low to move or power on, stop normal operation and prepare for manual charging handling as described in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Slide and transcript describing charger selection/disable context for manually charging a low-battery robot and the note that the robot cannot be turned on until taken to a charger.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot cannot be turned on and manual charging handling is required.
The observed battery behavior does not match the training guidance.
Additional support is needed to perform the manual charging handling described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Under 20% it will not accept any new task; at 15% it will turn off automatically and cannot be turned on until taken to a charger.

Slide text indicates charger selection and explains that if a charger is needed to manually charge a robot, the charger can be disabled so no other robot is sent to that charging station, after which the desired AGV can be pushed into the disabled charging station.

success_criteria: The operator can classify the robot battery state using the source thresholds.
The operator recognizes that under 20% the robot may not accept new tasks.
The operator recognizes that around 15% the robot may turn off automatically and cannot be turned on until taken to a charger.
The operator uses earlier charging judgment, such as around 30% when charging is available, to avoid time loss.
healthy_conditions: Battery percentage is available for review.
The robot remains sufficiently above the lower thresholds for normal tasking.
Charging is considered before the robot approaches the automatic shutdown threshold.
failure_conditions: Battery percentage cannot be determined from the available indication.
The robot is under 20% and may not accept new tasks.
The robot is around 15% and may turn off automatically.
The robot cannot be turned on until taken to a charger.
Observed battery behavior does not match the training guidance.
escalation_guidance: Escalate or seek additional support if the observed battery behavior does not match the training guidance.
Stop normal operation and prepare for manual charging handling if the robot is too low to move or power on.
Seek additional support if the battery reading method or recovery handling details needed are not defined in the source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Battery threshold discussion and manual charging context.
Nearby transcript reference to checking HV charge percentage in the power section.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Under 20% it will not accept any new task; at 15% it will turn off automatically and cannot be turned on until taken to a charger.
If you see something at 30% when it can charge, let's just because it will cost you more time later.

missing_details: The source packet does not define an exact screen, menu path, or standard method for reading battery percentage.
The source packet does not provide a formal logging or recording location for documenting low-battery risk.
The 30% guidance is conversational and not presented as a strict enforced threshold.
The packet references manual charging handling context but does not provide a complete step-by-step charging procedure in this candidate.
